answer:
Zoho Cliq’s implementation of the Model Context Protocol (MCP) enables AI agents to interact with Cliq data and workflows using standardized natural language instructions [1][2]. You can configure a Zoho Cliq MCP server through the official Zoho MCP console, which provides a unique, secure URL endpoint for your MCP client [3][4].

To integrate:
1. Create a server in the Zoho MCP console and select Zoho Cliq to add relevant tools [3][5].
2. Copy the generated MCP URL [3][5].
3. Add the URL to your AI client’s configuration (e.g., in VS Code via GitHub Copilot) to execute tasks like sending messages or checking activity [6][5].
